title: corymbia cadophora subsp. cadophora
intro: Corymbia cadophora subsp. cadophora, also known as the cadophora bloodwood, is a plant species native to Western Australia. This tree is valued for its timber and has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Corymbia cadophora subsp. cadophora are attributed to its high tannin content, which has been used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and are often used to promote overall health and well-being.
